### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:48:47 PM

It looks like Google Chrome is not installed on this system, so the browser automation tools can't connect to it. 

Would you like me to:
1. **Install Google Chrome** on this system so we can use the browser tools
2. **Use `curl`/`WebFetch`** to fetch the GitHub page content instead (no visual browser)

Which approach would you prefer?

---

### User
> 2/11/2026, 5:48:58 PM

1

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:49:00 PM





---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:49:01 PM

[
  {
    "type": "thinking",
    "thinking": "The user wants me to install Google Chrome. Let me do that on this Linux system.",
    "signature": ""
  }
]

---

### Assistant
> 2/11/2026, 5:49:02 PM

[
  {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01DZgPncG5ra3tyyxWvDFEPh",
    "name": "Bash",
    "input": {
      "command": "wget -q -O /tmp/google-chrome.deb https://dl.google.com/linux/direct/google-chrome-stable_current_amd64.deb 2>&1",
      "description": "Download Google Chrome .deb package",
      "timeout": 60000
    }
  }
]

---

> 2/11/2026, 5:49:39 PM

      "timeout": 120000
    }

]

| **Video** | 2 | Desktop code demo MP4 (partial content/206) |
| **API/Analytics** | 4 | `collector.github.com` and `api.github.com` stats |
